etymology.description: semantic 矢(矢) (shǐ, "arrow") + phonetic 委(委) (wěi) -> short (of stature)
etymology.interpretation: 矢(矢) (shǐ, "arrow") suggests something being straight and upright, while 委(委) (wěi) gives the sound. The use of an arrow, typically straight and long, juxtaposed in a context meaning 'short,' emphasizes a comparative sense of lower stature.

examples.chinese: 他的身材很矮(他的身材很矮)。
examples.english: He is very short in stature.

examples.chinese: 虽然他很矮(雖然他很矮)，但他在篮球队里表现出色(但他在籃球隊裏表現出色)。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán tā hěn ǎi, dàn tā zài lánqiú duì lǐ biǎoxiàn chūsè.
examples.english: Although he is short, he performs excellently in the basketball team.

examples.chinese: 那个女孩比她的妹妹矮一些(那個女孩比她的妹妹矮一些)。
examples.pinyin: Nàgè nǚhái bǐ tā de mèimei ǎi yīxiē.
examples.english: That girl is a bit shorter than her sister.
